sectionsUnavailable: Authenticated marketplace detail-page extraction remains pending for this package.
No calibrated skin-and-beauty trait model, dermatology model, cosmetic-response model, skin-aging model, acne model, UV-sensitivity model, or skin-cancer risk model is supplied with this fixture.
Skin type, UV sensitivity, cosmetic product response, acne, skin aging, dermatology outcomes, and skin-cancer risk are not inferred.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived HERC2/OCA2 rs12913832 AG call is available for pigmentation and eye-color context. This does not determine exact eye color, skin tone, skin type, UV sensitivity, dermatology status, or cosmetic outcome.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: OCA2 and HERC2 are important in pigmentation biology, but skin and eye color are influenced by multiple genes and non-genetic factors.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived MC1R rs1805007 CT call is available for pigmentation, hair-color, and freckle context. This is not dermatology guidance, skincare guidance, or a skin-cancer risk result.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: MC1R is a well-studied pigmentation gene, but many genes and non-genetic factors contribute to hair color, freckles, and related visible traits.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived EDAR hair-texture/thickness context row is available. This is educational context only and does not predict exact hair texture, cosmetic outcome, ancestry, or identity.
resultRows.sourceLabel: MedlinePlus Genetics: hair texture / DTC trait-test limits
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Hair texture and thickness have genetic contributions, but many genes and non-genetic factors can influence the trait.

resultRows.item: Skin Genes public sample PDF summary table
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The public Skin Genes sample PDF shows Skin and Beauty-relevant report structure: a summary table with trait, gene, common result, user result, risk, and impact columns for elasticity, hydration, skin glycation, skin inflammation, dark spots, and sagging eyelids.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: These rows document a related Sequencing.com/GeneInformed skin report format only. They must not be treated as this local fixture's skincare, dermatology, cosmetic, inflammation, skin-aging, UV-sensitivity, disease-risk, or treatment conclusions.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: MMP1 common GG, sample del/del; MMP3 common del/del, sample del/del; risk REDUCED; protective effect against wrinkle formation, sagging, and skin aging
sampleRows.description: The public sample PDF summary table shows Elasticity with MMP1 and MMP3 rows, reduced risk, and a protective impact statement.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Skin and Beauty sibling output-structure evidence. Extracted from page 3 of the public Skin Genes sample PDF summary table; this is observed sample output structure, not a local skincare conclusion.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: FLG common/sample rows GG, TT, AA, CC; AQP3 common AA, sample GG; risk ELEVATED; tendency for lowered skin lubrication, dryness, sun-radiation sensitivity, and skin allergies
sampleRows.description: The public sample PDF summary table shows Hydration and Moisture with FLG and AQP3 rows and elevated risk language.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Skin and Beauty sibling output-structure evidence. Extracted from page 3 of the public Skin Genes sample PDF summary table; recommendation details are sample content, not personal hydration or skincare advice.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: AGER common GG, sample GA; risk ELEVATED; increased glycation processes with added risk for wrinkles, sagging, and loss of radiance
sampleRows.description: The public sample PDF summary table shows Skin Glycation with AGER and elevated risk language.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Skin and Beauty sibling output-structure evidence. Extracted from page 3 of the public Skin Genes sample PDF summary table; this is observed sample output structure, not a local glycation or dermatology finding.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: ICAM1 AA, IL6 GG, IL1RN TT/TT, and IL1RL1 common GG, sample AA; risk ELEVATED; elevated IgE antibodies and increased inflammatory response of the skin
sampleRows.description: The public sample PDF summary table shows Skin Inflammation with ICAM1, IL6, IL1RN, and IL1RL1 rows and elevated risk language.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Skin and Beauty sibling output-structure evidence. Extracted from page 3 of the public Skin Genes sample PDF summary table; this is observed sample output structure, not a local inflammation diagnosis.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: SLC45A2 common CC, sample CC; risk NORMAL; no genetic risk addition
sampleRows.description: The public sample PDF summary table shows Dark Spots with SLC45A2 and normal risk language.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Skin and Beauty sibling output-structure evidence. Extracted from page 3 of the public Skin Genes sample PDF summary table; this is observed sample output structure, not a local dark-spot or dermatology conclusion.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: DLGAP1 common GG, sample GC; risk NORMAL; average rate of loss of elasticity around the eyes
sampleRows.description: The public sample PDF summary table shows Sagging Eyelids with DLGAP1 and normal risk language.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Skin and Beauty sibling output-structure evidence. Extracted from page 3 of the public Skin Genes sample PDF summary table; this is observed sample output structure, not a local eyelid or dermatology conclusion.

plainEnglishLimits: Visible traits are influenced by multiple genes and non-genetic factors.

missingInputs: Authenticated marketplace detail-page extraction is still pending for this package.
No calibrated skin-and-beauty trait probability model, dermatology model, cosmetic-response model, UV-sensitivity model, skin-aging model, acne model, or skin-cancer risk model is supplied.

Skin type, hydration, glycation, inflammation, dark spots, eyelid sagging, UV sensitivity, cosmetic product response, acne, skin aging, dermatology outcomes, and skin-cancer risk require separate validated evidence and are not inferred.
Many known and unknown contributors to pigmentation, freckles, hair texture, and other visible traits are not represented in this fixture.
uncertainty: Visible traits can be influenced by multiple genes, development, age, environment, and other non-genetic factors.
The Skin Genes sample rows are sibling output-structure evidence only, not personal Skin and Beauty findings.
A local agent should not quantify skin, beauty, dermatology, cosmetic-response, or skin-cancer risk probabilities without a calibrated model and applicability metadata.
limitations: This validation result uses derived evidence only; raw genome data is not included.

The report is educational and does not identify a person, infer race or ancestry, predict a child's appearance, give skincare guidance, diagnose skin conditions, or report melanoma or other skin-cancer risk.
Sample rows preserve public report structure only and are not local recommendations or local risk findings.
